changes.description: Backward-compatibility constraint surfaced for runtime_context_evaluator.py: existing *_disclosed=True keys at lines 237-242 are already consumed by active signals. Phase 4 additions MUST be additive — new answered_yes/answered_no/not_answered keys are added alongside, not instead of, existing keys. Prompt already says 'preserve existing disclosed-context semantics' but Cursor must be aware this is a strict regression requirement, not a preference.
changes.required_action: Cursor must verify existing test_runtime_context_evaluation.py passes unchanged against any new snapshot builder before adding new primitive keys.

changes.description: AAS exposure inference at runtime_context_evaluator.py lines 271-279 uses keyword matching on supplements list items ('steroid', 'prohormone', 'testosterone'). This is an existing gated behaviour. Phase 4 must preserve this path and not weaken it when adding answered_yes/answered_no supplement primitives.
changes.required_action: Add new supplement disclosure primitives alongside, not in place of, existing keyword-matching block.

changes.description: The 4 new governance artefacts (reusable_runtime_context_primitive_model_v1.yaml, context_questionnaire_contract_v1.yaml, batch2_full_coverage_activation_readiness_register_v1.yaml, batch2_medical_research_intake_contract_v1.yaml) must each explicitly set runtime_consumed: false to prevent accidental runtime ingestion. The runtime_context_requirements_model_v1.yaml is the ONLY runtime-consumed governance file.
changes.required_action: Each new governance artefact must contain runtime_consumed: false as a top-level field. Cursor must not forget this on any of the four files.

This is a behavioural change to analytical output pathways.

determinism_risks_surfaced: RISK 1: If Phase 4 adds new context keys that existing test fixtures do not account for, test_runtime_context_evaluation.py may fail. Cursor must update test fixtures in Phase 7 to cover new keys. Risk is LOW as additions are additive and do not change existing key values.

RISK 2: If build_runtime_context_snapshot is refactored rather than extended, existing *_disclosed=True behaviour may regress. Prompt says 'preserve existing disclosed-context semantics' — this is a MUST NOT regress constraint, not optional.

RISK 3: The context_questionnaire_contract_v1.yaml describes what the frontend MUST eventually provide. If Cursor treats this as a runtime contract and wires it into the evaluator before the frontend provides these fields, signals may suppress erroneously. The contract must remain governance-only (runtime_consumed: false).

hardening_notes: Sprint is correctly classified HIGH / BEHAVIOUR / TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. All 9 authority file paths verified to exist. Baseline state confirmed: ARCH-COMPLETION-1 and BATCH2-MINIMUM-COVERAGE-1 complete, AnalysisContext precedes signal evaluation, all 9 packages inactive with empty runtime_context_requirements. Key behavioural gap confirmed: build_runtime_context_snapshot() has no answered_yes/answered_no/not_answered semantics — this is the addressable gap the sprint targets. Three hardening notes added: (1) existing *_disclosed=True keys must be preserved — additive extension only; (2) AAS keyword inference must not be weakened; (3) all 4 new governance artefacts must set runtime_consumed: false to prevent accidental runtime ingestion. No STOP conditions anticipated to trigger from pre-existing state. GPT architectural review and human approval required before merge per HIGH risk route.
